subject: [PATCH v2 2/9] nfsd: hold rcu across localio cmpxchg retry

2026-06-02.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the NFS server (nfsd) where a freed nfsd_file object can be reused, leading to dereferencing of freed memory. The fix involves holding RCU (Read-Copy Update) lock across a cmpxchg retry block in the nfsd_open_local_fh function, ensuring that the slab page backing the nfsd_file is not recycled until the RCU grace period elapses. This prevents a non-zero word at the nf_ref offset of the recycled object from making the refcount bump appear to succeed, and subsequently dereferencing new->nf_net and new->nf_file out of freed memory.
